calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-30-2022 Off Calendar

Notice Of Default Judgment Prove-Up Hearing As To Defendant Carolyn White Doing Business As Jans Farm And Hay (Notices)
Off Calendar Sep-22-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for default judgment prove-up hearing as to Defendant Carolyn White doing business as Jans Farm and Hay.

Dept. 514; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:44:04 a.m. and concluding at 10:59:29 a.m.

Plaintiff Orion First Financial, L.L.C., a Washington Limited Liability Company represented by Mark M. Scott, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Carolyn White, an individual doing business as Jans Farm and Hay not appearing.

MATTHEW JACOBS is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.
Plaintiff proceed with an offer of proof.

Court examines MATTHEW JACOBS.
The Declaration of Matthew Jacobs in Support of Plaintiff's Request for Entry of Default Judgment Against Defendant Carolyn White doing business as Jans Farm and Hay filed Jul-21-2022 is MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE.

The matter is submitted.
The Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence to prove breach of service contract and the amount owing on the service contract. Court finds in favor of Plaintiff and awards the following judgment:

1. Damages: $78,513.60
2. Prejudgment Interest: $7,666.37
3. Attorney Fees: $1,056.33
4. Costs: $650.00

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $87,886.30
OTHER: Orion is to recover from Defendant Carolyn White possession of the following personal property: One (1) 2016 Terex-ASV Compact Track Loader ASVR35IA6WF00332; One (1) 2012 TH6000HS Tushogg Mulching Head TH0383. If Orion obtains possession of the personal property and sells it at a sale or sales, Orion shall apply the proceeds from any such sale or sales, net of any sale and recovery expenses, to reduce the amount owed by White under this judgment after such sale or sales has occured.

Proposed judgment signed and filed.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-17-2022 Continued To Dec-21-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
